home.title: pinacc640 — Independent Maker / AI Tools
home.hero_subtitle:
Independent developer. I build small tools, write notes, and share what I learn.
Currently focused on packaging AI capabilities into products that anyone can use.
home.card_ai_desc: Building small practical tools with multimodal models like Gemini and GPT. Latest: Xiaohongshu cover-card generator.
home.card_web3_desc: Researching quant strategies for prediction markets like Polymarket; building tools to automate trading workflows.
home.card_writing_desc: Turning my hands-on experiments into blog posts and tutorials, for people who want to build things themselves.
home.project_xhs_desc: Upload 3 product screenshots + a title; auto-generate 3 captions + 5 magazine-style cover cards (Swiss International style). Powered by Gemini 2.5 Flash + Playwright rendering, with dual-account free-tier API rotation. Open source, runs locally.
home.post3_excerpt: From cloning the repo to producing 5 magazine-style covers + 3 captions. Covers dual-key Gemini rotation, Playwright rendering, and the Swiss International layouts.

home.post2_excerpt: End-to-end guide to building a personal site from scratch. No coding required — just follow the steps.

project.title: Xiaohongshu Cover-Card Generator — pinacc640
project.page_subtitle: 3 screenshots → 3 captions + 5 magazine-style covers · dual Gemini key rotation · open source
project.card_intro_p1: Upload 3+ product screenshots + a title; the tool auto-generates 3 complete captions (title, body, tags) plus 5 cover cards in Swiss International style.
project.card_intro_p2_strong: All AI calls go through Gemini 2.5 Flash
project.card_intro_p2_rest: . Cover images are rendered locally by Playwright from HTML templates — no paid image API required.
project.feat_dual_rest: — switches automatically between two Gemini keys from two Google accounts. Doubles the free quota and avoids per-account rate limits.
project.feat_layouts_rest: — Swiss Grid / Full-bleed Hero / Serif Center / Dark Cinematic / Product Grid, rotated automatically to keep things fresh.
project.feat_copy_rest: — prompts are tuned to avoid the over-the-top influencer tone. Reads like a friend's recommendation.
project.feat_ui_rest: — Streamlit visual UI plus a command-line entry point.
project.feat_oss_rest: — MIT licensed. Runs locally; your data never leaves your machine.
project.card_stack_text: Python · Gemini 2.5 Flash · Streamlit · Playwright · Google Fonts CDN
project.card_flow_text: Title + screenshots → Gemini reads images and generates 3 captions → Playwright renders 5 layouts of HTML templates (with IKB-blue + grayscale filter on the screenshots) → outputs images + captions to a timestamped folder.
project.card_output_text: 3 captions (note_1/2/3.txt) + 5 covers (cover_A/B/C/D/E.png) + summary.json, all under output/timestamp_product/.

automation.title: Behind the Scenes — pinacc640
automation.page_subtitle: 100% AI-written · fully automated topic-picking & layout · I only do the final polish
automation.intro_title: This WeChat account is run by an AI
automation.intro_p1: Every article on the 棱镜PLSM WeChat account — from picking topics to writing, formatting and uploading drafts — is produced end-to-end by an AI agent orchestration system called openclaw 小龙虾.
automation.intro_p2: As the human in the loop, I do exactly two things: ① decide whether to publish, and ② tweak the tone in the final draft. The other 95% runs through the AI pipeline.
automation.intro_disclaimer: 📢 Full disclosure: the content you read is generated by AI, not written by a person.
automation.stack_lead: The whole system runs on my own machine. No commercial SaaS involved.
automation.stack_card_openclaw_desc: Locally-run AI agent orchestration tool. CLI-based, ships with workspace, agents, gateway, auth and hooks. The brain of the pipeline.
automation.stack_card_deepseek_desc: deepseek-chat (fast writing) + deepseek-reasoner (deep reasoning). 200k context window; the free tier easily covers dozens of articles a day.
automation.stack_card_hotspot_desc: Pulls Baidu Hot, Weibo Hot, Zhihu Hot daily, ranks by weight, and the AI auto-picks 3–5 candidates that fit the WeChat account's positioning.
automation.stack_card_writer_desc: Prompt tuned for WeChat reading habits: short paragraphs (≤3 sentences), heavy whitespace, strong emotional resonance, readability first. Output is publish-ready.
automation.stack_card_publisher_desc: Python script calls the official WeChat Open Platform API: uploads cover image to get media_id, formats HTML article, pushes to draft inbox.
automation.flow_lead: From a trending topic to a WeChat draft in under 90 seconds.
automation.flow_step1_desc: openclaw triggers the hot-topic agent, scans multi-platform trending boards, returns today's candidate list (with traffic estimates and sentiment scores).
automation.flow_step2_desc: AI matches the account's positioning (current affairs / cultural commentary), picks the best 1, generates a writing brief.
automation.flow_step3_desc: DeepSeek-Chat writes ~800 words from the brief + style guide, auto-formats with WeChat-friendly layout (one-or-two-sentence paragraphs, lots of whitespace).
automation.flow_step4_desc: Cover prompt feeds into the image generator, outputs a 900×500 standard WeChat banner image.
automation.flow_step5_desc: Script calls WeChat Draft API: upload cover → get media_id → push HTML article → arrives in draft inbox.
automation.flow_step6_desc: I open the WeChat dashboard, glance over it, tweak a word or two, hit publish. The whole manual step rarely takes more than 2 minutes.
automation.config_lead: Snippet from the actual ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json driving the pipeline:
automation.why_p1: Running a WeChat account traditionally is expensive and slow. One person can't sustain daily publishing; a team burns money.
automation.why_p2: I treated it as an engineering problem: orchestrate AI agents to make 「topic → write → format → publish」 a repeatable pipeline. Humans only sit at the final quality gate.
automation.why_p3: The result: one person + one laptop + under $15/month in API costs delivers what used to take a 3–5 person team.
automation.cta_title: Want to see it in action?
automation.cta_subtitle: Scan to follow 棱镜PLSM and see what 100% AI-written WeChat content actually reads like.
